document.title: Post-Market Clinical Follow-up Evaluation Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
authorityOrJurisdiction: Korea - MFDS Recall and suspension of sale
United Kingdom - MHRA Alerts and recalls for drugs and medical devices
standards: MDR Annex XIV, Part B – Post-market clinical follow-up
MDCG 2020-7 Post-market clinical follow-up (PMCF) Plan Template - A guide for manufacturers and notified bodies, April 2020
MDCG 2020-8 Post-market clinical follow-up (PMCF) Evaluation Report Template - A guide for manufacturers and notified bodies, April 2020

sourceSummary.oneLine: PMCF evaluation report for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW under MDR PMCF requirements.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Part of the clinical evaluation report and technical documentation for post-market clinical follow-up under MDR Annex XIV, Part B.
keyPoints: The document states PMCF is a continuous process that updates the clinical evaluation and shall be addressed in the manufacturer's post-market surveillance plan.
PMCF activities included surveys and screening scientific literature and other sources of clinical data.
No additional impact on clinical safety and performance was identified through PMCF activities.
No preventive and/or corrective measures were identified.
The device is Class IIb in accordance with rule 10 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

entities.name: Hur, Namkyu
entities.evidence: Approved by Hur, Namkyu QMR / PRRC March 25, 2025

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

concepts.name: Post-market clinical follow-up
concepts.evidence: The Medical Device Regulation (EU) 2017/745 (MDR) considers the post-market clinical follow-up (PMCF) as a continuous process that updates the clinical evaluation

concepts.name: PMCF evaluation report
concepts.evidence: document the results in this PMCF evaluation report that shall be part of the clinical evaluation report and the technical documentation

concepts.name: Class IIb device under MDR Annex VIII rule 10
concepts.evidence: Class IIb in accordance with rule 10 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745

concepts.name: Customer satisfaction survey
concepts.evidence: A total of 8 healthcare professionals/distributors were surveyed on customer satisfaction

concepts.name: Recall screening
concepts.evidence: FDA Recall 0; Korea - MFDS Recall and suspension of sale 0; United Kingdom - MHRA Alerts and recalls for drugs and medical devices 0

evidence.claim: The document is Technical Documentation Document No. A-PMCFR-5-001, Rev. No. 1.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-PMCFR-5-001 Rev. No. 1

evidence.claim: The PMCF evaluation report revision date is March 25, 2025.
evidence.evidenceText: Rev. Date March 25, 2025

evidence.claim: The report covers Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model name: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd. with SRN KR-MF-000020684.
evidence.evidenceText: 2.1 Manufacturer’s Name H&abyz Co., Ltd. 2.3 SRN KR-MF-000020684

evidence.claim: The authorised representative is JaviTech e.K. in Germany.
evidence.evidenceText: Authorised Representative name: JaviTech e.K. Address: SachsenhausenerStraße 16, 65824 Schwalbach amTaunus, Germany

evidence.claim: The device intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector intended purpose

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infants, pregnant women, fluoroscopy, or angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector contraindications

evidence.claim: The Basic UDI-DI is 88000704HAAP-27U.
evidence.evidenceText: 3.6 Basic UDI-DI 88000704HAAP-27U
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector UDI

evidence.claim: The EMDN code is Z11031180.
evidence.evidenceText: 3.14 EMDN EMDN Code Z11031180

evidence.claim: The device is Class IIb under MDR Annex VIII rule 10.
evidence.evidenceText: Class IIb in accordance with rule 10 of Annex VIII Regulation (EU) 2017/745

evidence.claim: The expected lifetime is 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: 3.16 Expected lifetime 5 years
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector expected lifetime

evidence.claim: PMCF activities were conducted as planned with no deviations from the PMCF plan.
evidence.evidenceText: The activities were conducted as planned. There were no deviations from the PMCF plan.

evidence.claim: The PMCF evaluation used surveys and screening scientific literature and other sources of clinical data.
evidence.evidenceText: We conducted PMCF evaluation using the following two methods set in the PMCF Plan... C.1: Surveys C.2: Screening scientific literature and other sources of clinical data

evidence.claim: No new critical performance or safety issues were found at this PMCF.
evidence.evidenceText: There were no new critical issues regarding the performance or safety of the device at this PMCF.

evidence.claim: Eight healthcare professionals/distributors were surveyed for customer satisfaction.
evidence.evidenceText: A total of 8 healthcare professionals/distributors were surveyed on customer satisfaction

evidence.claim: No additional risks related to product performance and safety were found through the survey.
evidence.evidenceText: No additional risks related to the performance and safety of the product were found through the survey.

evidence.claim: There were no customer complaints regarding the product during the survey period.
evidence.evidenceText: There were no customer complaints regarding the product during the survey period.

evidence.claim: No additional confirmed literature was found in scientific literature screening.
evidence.evidenceText: Literature search : There was no additional confirmed literature.

evidence.claim: FDA recall screening returned zero recalls for product code MQB for May 06, 2024 to December 31, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: FDA Recall 0 Advance search: May 06, 2024 to December 31, 2024 Keyword: MQB (Product Code)

evidence.claim: MFDS recall and suspension of sale screening returned zero results for keyword 의료영상획득장치 for May 06, 2024 to December 31, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Korea - MFDS Recall and suspension of sale 0 Advance search: May 06, 2024 to December 31, 2024 Keyword: 의료영상획득장치

evidence.claim: MHRA alerts and recalls screening returned zero results for keyword x-ray detector for May 06, 2024 to December 31, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: United Kingdom - MHRA Alerts and recalls for drugs and medical devices 0 Advance search: May 06, 2024 to December 31, 2024 Keyword: x-ray detector

evidence.claim: The product was stated to have equivalent clinical performance to DRX-1, CXDI-70C, and CXDI-31 through the Clinical Evaluation Report.
evidence.evidenceText: Our product was confirmed to have equivalent clinical performance to DRX-1 through the Clinical Evaluation Report... equivalent clinical performance to CXDI-70C... equivalent clinical performance to CXDI-31

evidence.claim: The PMCF report references CER A-TCF-5-118 Rev.4 and Risk Management Report A-TCF-5-104 Rev.6.
evidence.evidenceText: Refer to A-TCF-5-118 Clinical Evaluation Report (Rev.4)... Refer to A-TCF-5-104 Risk Management Report (Rev.6)

evidence.claim: The overall PMCF conclusion was no additional impact on clinical safety and performance and no preventive or corrective measures.
evidence.evidenceText: No additional impact on clinical safety and performance was identified through PMCF activities. 8.2 Need for preventive and/or corrective measures None

evidence.claim: The first PMCF report date was December 31, 2024, with the second report planned for December 2025 and annual timeline.
evidence.evidenceText: Reported date of the first report: December 31, 2024 Planned date of the second report: December 2025 Timeline: Once a year
